name: ParallelizerUSDp — Main Diamond
notes: Main diamond proxy. Fork of Angle Transmuter. Delegates to all facets. Highest value target. Diamond proxy storage — check slot collision between facets.
attackAngle: Diamond storage collision, delegatecall hijacking, facet upgrade access control, protocol insolvency via mint/burn manipulation

name: Parallelizer Swapper Facet
notes: Mint and burn logic for USDp. Adaptive fees. Primary attack surface for stablecoin depeg via fee manipulation or oracle bypass.
attackAngle: Oracle manipulation, adaptive fee bypass, flash loan mint/burn arbitrage, rounding errors in fee calculation

name: Parallelizer Redeemer Facet
notes: Redemption logic — users burn USDp for basket assets. Proportional redemption mechanics. Check reserve accounting and slippage.
attackAngle: Reserve drain via redemption, proportional accounting errors, slippage exploitation, reentrancy on asset transfer

name: FlashParallel — Flashloan Module
notes: Flash loan module. Historically rich attack surface — flash loans are the entry vector for most DeFi exploits. Check fee enforcement, reentrancy, and repayment validation.
attackAngle: Flash loan fee bypass, reentrancy via callback, repayment validation bypass, combined flash loan + swap attack

name: USDp Token
notes: USDp stablecoin ERC20. Minted by Parallelizer. Check minting access control, burn logic, permit() implementation, supply cap.
attackAngle: Unauthorized minting, access control on mint/burn, ERC20 permit() replay, infinite approval exploitation

name: sUSDp — Savings Module
notes: Savings/yield vault for USDp. ERC4626 likely. Check share price manipulation, deposit/withdraw rounding, and yield harvesting logic.
attackAngle: Share price inflation attack, deposit/withdraw rounding exploit, yield harvesting front-running, vault insolvency

name: SettersGovernor Facet
notes: Governance parameter setters. Any unprotected setter is a direct path to protocol insolvency — collateral ratios, fee parameters, oracle addresses.
attackAngle: Unprotected parameter setters, timelock bypass, governance role escalation, collateral ratio manipulation

name: ParallelAccessManager
notes: Central access control contract. Gates all privileged calls across the protocol. If this is compromised everything is. Check role assignment, escalation paths, and admin transfer logic.
attackAngle: Role escalation, admin transfer to zero address, missing role checks, cross-contract privilege bypass
